annotations.label: Riemann sphere (extended complex plane)

anchor.snippet: is a model of the extended complex plane

annotations.note: Mathlib's general one-point compactification `OnePoint ℂ` is the topological model of the extended plane, but there is no dedicated Riemann-sphere object carrying its complex structure.

annotations.label: Extended complex numbers

anchor.snippet: consist of the complex numbers

annotations.note: The set ℂ ∪ {∞} is exactly `OnePoint ℂ`, a special case of the generic `OnePoint` construction.

annotations.label: Riemann sphere as set of extended complex numbers

anchor.snippet: is referred to as the Riemann sphere

annotations.note: `OnePoint ℂ` represents this set, but Mathlib does not name it the Riemann sphere.

annotations.label: Addition on extended complex numbers

anchor.snippet: Addition of complex numbers may be extended by defining

annotations.note: `OnePoint ℂ` carries no `Add` instance, so extended addition is not formalized.

annotations.label: Multiplication on extended complex numbers

anchor.snippet: and multiplication may be defined by

annotations.note: `OnePoint ℂ` carries no `Mul` instance, so extended multiplication is not formalized.

annotations.label: Extended complex numbers are not a field

anchor.snippet: the extended complex numbers do not form a field

annotations.note: With no algebraic structure on `OnePoint ℂ`, this non-field statement is absent.

annotations.label: Division on extended complex numbers

anchor.snippet: it is customary to define division on

annotations.note: `OnePoint ℂ` has no `Div` instance, so extended division is not formalized.

annotations.label: Rational functions extend continuously to the sphere

anchor.snippet: can be extended to a continuous function on the Riemann sphere

annotations.note: No continuous extension of rational functions to `OnePoint ℂ` is formalized.

annotations.label: Rational functions are all holomorphic self-maps

anchor.snippet: form all possible holomorphic functions from the Riemann sphere to itself

annotations.note: The sphere lacks a complex-manifold structure in Mathlib, so this classification of holomorphic self-maps is absent.

annotations.label: Extending a specific rational function

anchor.snippet: For example, given the function

annotations.note: No worked extension of a specific rational function to the sphere appears in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Transition map and Riemann sphere as complex manifold

anchor.snippet: is called the transition map between the two copies

annotations.note: Mathlib gives `OnePoint ℂ` no two-chart complex atlas with transition map z ↦ 1/z.

annotations.label: Riemann surface

anchor.snippet: this is also called a Riemann surface

annotations.note: General complex-manifold infrastructure (charted spaces / `IsManifold` over ℂ) exists, but there is no dedicated Riemann-surface (1-dim complex manifold) definition.

annotations.label: Sphere as one-point compactification of the plane

anchor.snippet: the resulting space is the one-point compactification

annotations.note: This gives a homeomorphism `OnePoint V ≃ₜ sphere` for any finite-dim real space V, with the plane ℂ ≅ ℝ² as a special case.

annotations.label: Uniformization theorem

anchor.snippet: states that every simply-connected Riemann surface is biholomorphic

annotations.note: The uniformization theorem is not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Unique complex structure on the 2-sphere

anchor.snippet: the two-dimensional sphere admits a unique complex structure

annotations.note: The 2-sphere only has a real smooth structure in Mathlib; uniqueness of its complex structure is not formalized.

annotations.label: Complex projective line

anchor.snippet: can also be defined as the complex projective line

annotations.note: Mathlib proves `OnePoint K ≃ ℙ K (Fin 2 → K)`, identifying the extended line with the projective line `Projectivization`, with K = ℂ the projective line ℙ¹(ℂ).

annotations.label: Lines and conics biholomorphic to the projective line

anchor.snippet: is biholomorphic to the complex projective line

annotations.note: No biholomorphism of plane lines/conics with ℙ¹(ℂ) is formalized.

annotations.label: Sphere via stereographic projection

anchor.snippet: can be visualized as the unit sphere

annotations.note: `stereographic` defines the stereographic projection partial homeomorphism for the unit sphere in any inner-product space, generalizing the ℂ/ℝ³ visualization.

annotations.label: Unit sphere diffeomorphic to Riemann sphere

anchor.snippet: the unit sphere is diffeomorphic to the Riemann sphere

annotations.note: Mathlib establishes a homeomorphism `OnePoint ℂ ≃ₜ sphere`, but not a diffeomorphism to a complex Riemann sphere.

annotations.label: Unit circles and equator are identified

anchor.snippet: the equator of the unit sphere are all identified

annotations.note: This specific identification of unit circles with the sphere's equator under stereographic projection is not formalized.

annotations.label: Complex structure determines metric up to conformal equivalence

anchor.snippet: does uniquely determine a metric up to conformal equivalence

annotations.note: Mathlib has no theory relating complex structures and conformal classes of metrics.

annotations.label: Correspondence between complex structures and conformal classes

anchor.snippet: in one-to-one correspondence with conformal classes of metrics

annotations.note: This bijection between complex structures and conformal metric classes is not formalized.

annotations.label: Existence of complete constant-curvature metric

anchor.snippet: there is always a complete metric with constant curvature

annotations.note: Existence of complete constant-curvature metrics in a conformal class is not formalized.

annotations.label: Gauss–Bonnet forces positive curvature

anchor.snippet: the Gauss–Bonnet theorem implies that a constant-curvature metric

annotations.note: The Gauss–Bonnet theorem is not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Round metric on the Riemann sphere

anchor.snippet: chart on the Riemann sphere, the metric with

annotations.note: The round (spherical) metric on the Riemann sphere is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Agreement with Fubini–Study metric

anchor.snippet: this metric agrees with the standard Fubini–Study metric

annotations.note: The Fubini–Study metric is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Unique metric with 3-dimensional isometry group

anchor.snippet: the only metric on the sphere whose group of orientation-preserving isometries is 3-dimensional

annotations.note: This characterization via a 3-dimensional isometry group is not formalized.

annotations.label: Any metric conformally equivalent to round metric

anchor.snippet: is conformally equivalent to the round metric

annotations.note: No statement that every metric on the sphere is conformal to the round metric appears in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Automorphism of the Riemann sphere

anchor.snippet: an automorphism is an invertible conformal map

annotations.note: Mathlib defines the GL(2,K) Möbius action on `OnePoint K`, but not the notion of an automorphism as an invertible conformal map of the sphere.

annotations.label: Automorphisms are exactly Möbius transformations

anchor.snippet: the only such maps are the Möbius transformations

annotations.note: Mathlib does not prove that every conformal self-map of the sphere is a Möbius transformation.

annotations.label: Möbius transformations as compositions

anchor.snippet: any Möbius transformation can be written as a composition of these

annotations.note: No decomposition of Möbius transformations into translations, dilations and inversion is formalized.

annotations.label: Group of Möbius transformations is PGL

anchor.snippet: The group of Möbius transformations is the projective linear group

annotations.note: The GL(2,K) action by Möbius transformations on `OnePoint K` is defined, but its identification with PGL₂ is not established.

annotations.label: Isometries form a subgroup isomorphic to SO(3)

anchor.snippet: The isometries form a proper subgroup

annotations.note: No isomorphism between the sphere's orientation-preserving isometries and SO(3) is formalized.

annotations.label: Meromorphic function

anchor.snippet: a meromorphic function on the complex plane

annotations.note: `MeromorphicOn` (built on `MeromorphicAt`) defines meromorphy over any normed field, with the complex plane as a special case, though the reinterpretation as holomorphic maps to the sphere is not formalized.

annotations.label: No non-constant holomorphic maps on compact surfaces

anchor.snippet: there are no non-constant holomorphic maps to the complex numbers

annotations.note: Mathlib proves any complex-differentiable function on a compact preconnected complex manifold is constant, generalizing the compact-Riemann-surface case.
